title: Fiscal Health

metrics.label: Congress Borrowing (15 months)
metrics.context: BRS took a decade to borrow Rs 2.8 lakh crore

narrative: State debt is projected to reach Rs 5,04,814 crore by end of 2025-26. The Congress government borrowed Rs 1,52,918 crore in its first 15 months — a pace that exceeds BRS's borrowing trajectory (Rs 2.8 lakh crore over a decade). Revenue surplus collapsed from Rs 5,944 crore in 2022-23 to Rs 297 crore in the 2024-25 budget estimate. GST growth is near-flat (0-1%) against a national average of 9-10%. The six guarantee schemes consume Rs 56,000 crore annually from the budget, while Kaleswaram project debt servicing costs approximately Rs 10,000 crore per year.
